text.title: Bounded generator-critic loop with three exit conditions
text.summary: Cap reflection at 3 iterations; exit on critic veto, score plateau, or cap; use a cheaper critic for rubric tasks.

section.title: Three exit conditions, in priority order
section.p: An unbounded reflection loop is the single most common reason production agents overspend. Bounding by iteration count alone is too rigid — the critic should be allowed to short-circuit when satisfied, and a plateau detector kills runs that stop improving.

rule.statement: Generator-Critic loops must exit on the first true condition: (1) critic returns should_continue=False, (2) score delta vs the prior iteration is below epsilon for 2 consecutive iterations, (3) iteration count reaches the hard cap (default 3, never above 5).
rule.rationale: Empirical reflection studies (LangChain Reflection Agents) show iteration 2 captures most of the lift, iteration 3 adds the long tail, and iterations 4+ contribute below the noise floor. Three independent stop conditions cover correctness (critic veto), efficiency (plateau), and safety (cap).

example.code: output = generate(prompt) prev_score = -1 for i in range(MAX_ITERS := 3): critique = critic(output, prompt) # structured: score + should_continue if not critique.should_continue: break if i > 0 and abs(critique.score - prev_score) < EPSILON: break output = generate(prompt, feedback=critique.feedback) prev_score = critique.score return output
example.note: Cap, veto, and plateau all checked. Loop exits early when any one fires.

example.code: while not critic(output).is_perfect(): output = generate(prompt, feedback=critic(output).feedback)
example.note: No cap, no plateau detection — perfect-is-enemy-of-good drives unbounded spend.

antipattern.description: Letting the generator and critic share the same model and temperature.
antipattern.reason: Shared prior — generator and critic make the same mistakes, critic rubber-stamps bad output. Either swap models, swap temperatures, or use a rubric prompt that forces a different reasoning path.

antipattern.description: Critic returns only a numeric score; loop decides should_continue from a fixed threshold.
antipattern.reason: The critic has more context than the threshold rule. Force the critic to emit should_continue explicitly so domain logic stays in the prompt, not in caller code.

section.reference: Pydantic schema for the critic's structured output (score, should_continue, feedback).
Reference loop implementing all three exit conditions.
LangChain — Reflection Agents (empirical anchor for the 3-iteration cap).
Self-correcting RAG agent in LangGraph — bounded loop in production.

list.item: Rubric criticism (style, format, completeness, presence of required sections) — use a cheap model (Haiku 4.5, gpt-4.1-mini, Llama-3.3-70B). Constrained classification, no reasoning needed.
Correctness criticism (math, code, citation accuracy) — use the same tier as the generator. Catching wrong answers requires the same capacity that produces them.
Mixed criticism — split into two critics: cheap-rubric runs first, strong-correctness only when rubric passes. Cuts critic cost ~60%.
